role_delineation_check: Social Worker (SW) manages HHA selection, DME orders for the prosthetic/wheelchair, and family dispute resolution. Patient Navigator (PN) audits Atlantis demographics, verifies family sentiment, and flags transition barriers to the SW.
demographic_audit_note: Audit of Face Sheet against Atlantis queue revealed a discrepancy: Atlantis listed a disconnected landline, while the Face Sheet provided the daughter’s mobile number. Insurance verified as Straight Medicare.
home_vs_ltc_goal: Home (per SW assessment, despite son's preference for LTC).
weekly_staff_update: Week 2: SW reports patient is progressing with prosthetic training. SW notes high friction between daughter (pro-home) and son (pro-LTC). PN flagged the 'Sentiment_Readiness_Gap' to the SW.
v_card_flyer_status: V-Card successfully inserted into patient's smartphone for Caller ID recognition; program flyer delivered to the daughter during bedside visit.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Reached daughter 24 hours pre-discharge. She reports being 'Anxious' (Sentiment Score: 2/5) due to her brother's refusal to help with ramp installation. Flagged to SW.
atlantis_final_sync: D/C date of 11/14 and first MA visit date of 11/15 documented in Atlantis after SW confirmed HHA acceptance.
ma_visit_booking: MA visit scheduled for 11/15 at 11:00 AM. Booking was held until SW confirmed HHA 'Accepted' status via the Atlantis portal.
narrative_summary: PN audited patient demographics and corrected the primary contact number in Atlantis to ensure successful reach rates. During the maintenance phase, PN verified a significant sentiment gap between the patient's children and flagged this friction to the SW for a care conference. Once the SW confirmed the HHA was ready and the family disagreement was stabilized, the PN scheduled the first MA visit within the 24-hour window.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis demographic audit reveals outdated contact number.
Son demands LTC placement while daughter prepares the home.
HHA portal status remains 'Pending' 48 hours before discharge.

format_2_triples.situation: Patient's son expresses 'Caregiver Panic' and wants to cancel the home transition.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Documented the son's concerns and Flagged the sentiment score to the SW for a family conference.

format_2_triples.situation: Daughter is unsure of who will arrive at the home on Day 1.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the daughter on the roles of the HHA nurse versus the Healing Partners MA.

format_2_triples.situation: HHA has not confirmed the start-of-care date in the portal.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verified the status in Atlantis and Asked the SW for an update on HHA readiness.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the SW to finalize the family mediation and HHA selection before providing the daughter with the appointment card.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's authority over family dynamics and prevents providing premature scheduling information.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the daughter's readiness via a pulse call, document the son's refusal to participate as a barrier, and flag the sentiment score to the SW in Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Follows the 'Liaison' model by documenting data and flagging barriers without interfering in the mediation itself.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the HHA intake coordinator to ask if they can provide a social worker to help the family resolve their disagreement so discharge can stay on track.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Violates the No-Vendor Rule by contacting HHA intake and attempts to 'Fix' a family problem that belongs to the facility SW.
